meta.title: U14 Girls Academy — Individual Training Programme
meta.description: Complete individual training programme for a U14 girls academy player. All sessions designed for individual practice. Includes shooting, passing, dribbling, FIFA 11+, strength, speed, rhythmic movement, and football intelligence.
notes: Sessions are designed for individual practice — no team or coach required for most drills
Knee alignment warning applies to all relevant exercises (ACL prevention priority for U14 females)
FIFA 11+ must be performed at least twice per week to be effective
Rest times should be respected — quality over quantity

diagrams.conventions: viewBox='0 0 220 [h]' for all diagrams. All colours are literal hex — no CSS vars — so SVGs are fully portable. Arrowhead marker id='arr' is defined inside every SVG defs block.

activation.name: Light touch
activation.detail: Soft passes against wall × 10 each foot

drills.name: Top 5m box — centre
drills.execution: Place cone on bottom line of 5m box centre. Push ball then alternate shooting left corner (left foot) and right corner (right foot).
cues: Cone on the BOTTOM line of the 5m box — not floating inside
Small push of the ball first, then strike
Alternate left corner (L foot) and right corner (R foot)
Shot lines go INTO the goal corners — not outside the posts

diagram.label: 5m box — centre cone, alternate corners

drills.name: 5m box — right cone

cones.label: Right corner cone
cones.position: right corner of 5m box bottom line

drills.execution: Cone at right corner of 5m box. Right foot shoots into left corner.
cues: Cone at the RIGHT corner of the 5m box bottom line
Strike with RIGHT foot into the LEFT corner
Drive through the ball — locked ankle

drills.name: Penalty spot V-shape

cones.label: V-shape
cones.position: V-shape at top of penalty spot

drills.execution: 3 cones in V-shape at penalty spot. Player starts at 16m line. Perform skill move then alternate shots: left foot to left corner, right foot to right corner. Walk back between shots.
cues: V-shape cones at penalty spot
Player starts at the 16m line
Shots from OUTSIDE edges of side cones
Alternate: left foot → left corner, right foot → right corner
Walk back to recover between shots

drills.name: Thierry Henry — left

cones.label: Curve cones
cones.position: curve shape 2–3m to the RIGHT of penalty spot, top cone one step BELOW spot level

drills.execution: 4 cones in a curve to the RIGHT of the penalty spot. Push ball straight past inside of top cone, run OUTSIDE the curve, strike with LEFT foot to LEFT corner. Open hips on finish.
cues: 4 cones in a curve to the RIGHT of spot
Top cone is one step BELOW spot level
Push ball straight past the INSIDE of top cone
Run OUTSIDE the curve — not through it
Strike at intersection of ball line and run arc
LEFT foot → LEFT corner · open hips

drills.name: Thierry Henry — right

cones.label: Curve cones
cones.position: curve shape 2–3m to the LEFT of penalty spot, top cone one step BELOW spot level

drills.execution: Mirror of D2. Cones to LEFT of spot. Push ball with left foot, run outside the curve, strike with RIGHT foot to RIGHT corner.
cues: 4 cones in a curve to the LEFT of spot
Push ball with LEFT foot past inside of top cone
Run OUTSIDE the curve
RIGHT foot → RIGHT corner · open hips

activation.name: Dynamic stretch
activation.detail: High knees, heel flicks, hip openers × 15m

activation.name: Light finishing
activation.detail: 5 easy finishes to warm up shooting mechanics

drills.name: 16m box — defender wall

cones.label: Defender/wall cones
cones.position: horizontal line at top of 16m box

drills.execution: 3 cones on 16m line simulating a wall/defender. Perform skill move then shoot from outside edges of cones. Alternate left and right foot.
cues: 3 cones on the 16m line — simulate a defensive wall
Shots from OUTSIDE edges of the side cones
Alternate: left foot → left corner, right foot → right corner
Strike with pace and accuracy

drills.execution: 3 cones horizontal, 1m outside the 16m box on right side. Strike point is ON the 16m line. Perform skill move, approach, and curl LEFT foot to far post (left corner).

Open hips to generate curl

activation.name: Light touch
activation.detail: Soft passes against wall × 10 each foot

drills.name: Close range passing
drills.execution: Stand 1.5–2m from wall. Pass rhythmically to the middle target. 3 sets left foot, 3 sets right foot, 3 sets both feet alternating.
cues: Stand 1.5–2m from rebounder or wall
Hit the middle target consistently — same spot every time
Find a rhythm — pass, receive, pass, receive
3 sets left foot · 3 sets right foot · 3 sets both feet

drills.execution: Stand 1.5–2m from wall. Strike with laces — locked ankle. 3 sets each foot.
cues: Stand 1.5–2m from rebounder or wall
Lock ankle firm before contact — no floppy foot
Strike with laces, follow through toward the wall
3 sets left foot · 3 sets right foot · 3 sets both feet

drills.name: Medium range passing
drills.execution: Stand 3m from wall. 1 touch to control, then pass immediately on second touch. Lean forward over the ball.
cues: Stand 3m from rebounder or wall
1 touch to control, then pass immediately on the second touch
Lean body forward over the ball — no leaning back
Count only clean target touches as a make

drills.name: Medium range one-touch passing

cues: 3m from wall — one touch only, no control step
Lean slightly forward, keep weight balanced over the ball
Laces side contact — instep/laces
3 sets left · 3 sets right · 3 sets both feet

drills.name: Pass through gates (180° turn)
drills.execution: Wall 1.5–2m behind. 3 gates 3m in front. Pass back, receive return, 180° turn, pass through a gate. Alternate gates G1→G2→G3.
cues: Wall 1.5–2m behind · 3 gates 3m in front
① Pass back to wall · ② receive return · ③ turn 180° · ④ pass through gate
Alternate gates each rep — don't repeat the same gate twice
Only clean passes between both cones count as a make

drills.name: Low driven pass through gates
sequence: ④ Low driven laces strike through gate
drills.execution: Same as D1 but final pass is a low driven laces strike. Ball must stay on ground through the gate.
cues: Same setup as D1 — wall behind, 3 gates in front
④ Low driven laces — ball must stay on the ground
Alternate gates each rep — only grounded passes count

drills.name: Long passes into boxes
drills.execution: 2 boxes on the ground. Near at 10m, far at 12m with +1m offset on both axes. Alternate near→far→near→far every rep.
cues: Near box at 10m · far box at 12m
Far box offset +1m on both x and y axes
Alternate every rep: near → far → near → far
Ball must land and stay inside the box to count

sessions.title: Dribbling Skills Session
sessions.note: 6 modules — pick one per training day. Each module has its own progress test.

activation.name: Light dribble
activation.detail: Slow walk through 10 cones, both feet

drills.name: Straight 10
cues: Body direction follows ball direction at all times
Head down OR head up scanning — practise both
If ball touches a cone — don't stop, keep going

diagram.label: Straight 10 — weave through 10 cones 1 foot apart

drills.name: Zigzag

setup.offset: L2 cone at midpoint of L1 gaps

cues: 10 cones — 2 offset parallel lines of 5
2 feet vertical · 1 foot horizontal · L2 at mid of L1 gaps
Touch don't stop — keep momentum through errors

diagram.label: Zigzag — 2 offset lines of 5 cones
diagram.svg:

drills.name: Ronaldinho
cues: Densely packed random cones — no fixed path
React and redirect — simulate tight space dribbling
Full intensity for 15s, complete rest for 25s

cues: Head up scanning throughout
Goal: close gap vs T1 to under 1 second

drills.name: Speed turns

Explode out of every turn

skill_move_cues.single_stepover: Shoulder drops in fake direction — plant foot beside ball, step over, explode the other way
skill_move_cues.double_stepover: First step-over goes in the direction you'll actually take
skill_move_cues.body_feint: Drop the glute, commit weight convincingly to the fake side
skill_move_cues.reverse_stepover: Step over then roll back — misdirection then acceleration

modules.label: Anticipation Dribbling
modules.app_recommendation: Seconds Pro (iOS/Android) — random beep intervals 3–8 seconds
modules.chime_rule: Random chime sounds → burst faster for next 3 cones. React instantly.

drills.name: Elite 10 straight
drills.chime_action: On chime: burst faster for next 3 cones, then return to normal pace
cues: 10 cones · 1 foot apart · dribble at normal pace
Random chime → burst faster for next 3 cones
React instantly — no hesitation between chime and burst

tests.name: Reaction burst test
tests.description: Record time from chime to passing the 3rd burst cone
tests.scoring: Average the middle 3 times (discard fastest and slowest)
cues: 5 attempts · average the 3 middle times
Tracks reaction speed week over week

sessions.title: FIFA 11+ Injury Prevention Session
sessions.key_cue: Knees must NEVER buckle inward — primary ACL risk factor for teenage female players

parts.label: Part 1 — Running
parts.description: Slow-speed running with active stretching. Players run in pairs.

exercises.name: Jogging straight ahead
cues: Jog together to last cones

Relaxed stride

parts.label: Part 3 — High Speed Running
parts.description: 4 exercises at moderate to fast pace. Use same cone corridor as Part 1.

exercises.name: Forward-backward running
cues: Run 2 cone pairs forward · run 1 backward
Small quick steps on backward phase
Hips and knees slightly bent when running backward

exercises.name: Bounding
cues: 6–8 high bounding steps then jog
Land on ball of foot with knee bent · spring off
Do NOT let knee buckle inward

     transform="translate(-79.75,-57)" />
</svg>

exercises.name: Sprinting
cues: Sprint at full speed to last cones
Jog back easily to recover

diagram.label: Sprinting

exercises.name: Iso hold chin-up
exercises.tempo_description: Jump to top position (0s) · hold chin above bar for 10 seconds · lower under control (0s)
cues: Jump or step to get chin above bar — don't kip up
Hold: elbows bent, shoulders down and back, chest up
Body straight — no swinging or arching
If can't hold 10s, reduce to 5s and build up

exercises.name: Elevated push-ups
exercises.tempo_description: Lower chest to box slowly (2–3s) · pause 1s at bottom · push up EXPLOSIVELY
cues: Hands on elevated surface · body straight head to heels
Lower chest under control · push up with maximum power
Elbows at ~45° — not flared wide
Body must stay rigid — no sagging hips

exercises.name: Med ball chest pass
exercises.tempo_description: Ball to chest (1s) · push from chest controlled (1s) · partner catches and absorbs (1s)
cues: Hold ball at chest height before each pass
Push from chest · extend arms fully · step into the pass
Catch with soft hands — absorb the ball
Feet shoulder width · slight knee bend throughout

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.tempo_description: Get into position slowly · hold for 30–60s · release with control. Progress: add 5s each session.
cues: Elbows directly under shoulders · forearms flat
Straight line from head to heel — pull stomach and glutes in
Breathe normally — don't hold breath
No sagging hips · no raised buttocks · stop if form breaks

exercises.name: Bodyweight squat
exercises.tempo_description: Lower for 3 seconds · hold at bottom for 2 seconds · stand up fast (0 = no pause at top)
cues: Feet shoulder width · toes slightly out
Chest up · back straight · lean torso slightly forward
Lower until thighs parallel to floor (90°) · drive through heels to rise
Knees must track over toes — never buckle inward

exercises.name: Lunge
exercises.tempo_description: Step forward (1s) · lower back knee toward floor (2s) · drive up through front heel (1s) · return (1s)
cues: Step forward — front foot flat · back heel raised
Lower back knee toward floor — stop 2–3cm short of touching
Front knee stays above ankle — not past the toes
Torso stays upright — don't lean forward

exercises.name: Sidelying leg raises
exercises.tempo_description: Raise top leg slowly (2s) · hold 1s at top · lower slowly (2s)
cues: Lie on side · body in straight line · support head on lower arm
Raise top leg to 30–45° · toes pointing forward, not ceiling
Keep pelvis still — don't let hips rotate backward
No swinging — controlled up AND down equally

exercises.name: Lateral lunge hold
exercises.tempo_description: Step wide (1s) · lower into lateral lunge slowly (2s) · hold 10–20s · return (1s)
cues: Wide step to the side · bend stepping knee · other leg stays straight
Toes of straight leg point forward · heel stays on floor
Chest up · push hips back and down — not forward
Bent knee must track over toes — not caving inward

sessions.title: Speed Training
sessions.note: Always do Block 1 → Block 2 → Block 3 in order. Never sprint cold.

blocks.label: Block 1 — Wall Mechanics
blocks.description: All exercises use a wall at ~45° lean. Same body angle throughout, increasing speed.

exercises.name: Speed mechanic position
cues: Arms-length from wall · hands flat · lean at ~45°
Rigid straight line from head to heel
Drive knee up to 90° · foot dorsiflexed
Hold position — this is the sprint start position
No sagging hips — glutes and core tight

Drive knee high · body rigid · hold 10 seconds

exercises.name: Wall march
cues: Alternate legs in marching action
Drive knee up to 90° then stomp down as other rises
Controlled rhythm — focus on position, not speed
Foot lands under the hip — not in front

cues: Start from march position
Quickly switch: up leg down, down leg up
Faster than the march — speed mechanics begin here
Both legs pass through same 90° knee position

exercises.name: Wall run
cues: Same 45° position — run in place at maximum speed
Drive knees up hard and switch as fast as possible
Land on ball of foot — no flat-footed slap
Body position must NOT change at high speed

blocks.label: Block 2 — Plyometrics
blocks.description: All 5 exercises train ground contact stiffness and reactive power. Land on ball of foot, minimum ground contact time.
blocks.key_cue: Minimum ground contact time — not maximum height. Ball of foot. Stiff ankle.

exercises.name: Pogo jumps
cues: Jump straight up and down — no forward movement
Ankles stiff like springs — minimal knee bend
Arms loose — no big swing
Land on ball of foot · bounce off immediately

diagram.label: Pogo jumps — straight up and down, stiff ankles
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='100' x2='210' y2='100' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><path d='M30,96 Q36,60 42,96' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M42,96 Q48,60 54,96' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M54,96 Q60,60 66,96' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M66,96 Q72,60 78,96' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.8'/><circle cx='60' cy='46' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='60' y1='53' x2='60' y2='72' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='60' y1='72' x2='54' y2='90' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='60' y1='72' x2='66' y2='90' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='54' cy='94' r='4' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2'/><circle cx='66' cy='94' r='4' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='140' y='50' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>straight up/down</text><text x='140' y='70' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>ankles stiff</text><text x='140' y='80' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>like springs</text><text x='140' y='95' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>min. ground time</text></svg>

exercises.name: Forward / back jumps
cues: Jump forward over line · land · immediately jump back
Reactive — loading for next jump on landing
Same ankle stiffness as pogo

exercises.name: Lateral jumps
cues: Jump side to side over line — both feet together
Land softly · immediately rebound
Knees must not buckle inward on landing

Quick and rhythmic — same ankle spring
Land softly on wide position

exercises.name: Split stance quick switch
cues: Start in split stance · jump · switch feet in air
Arms opposite to legs — running arm action
Land with soft knees — no straight-leg landings

blocks.label: Block 3 — Sprints
blocks.description: Apply everything from Blocks 1 and 2. Full recovery between sprints — this is speed work, not conditioning.
start_variations: Standing start — feet staggered, lean forward on go signal
Athletic stance — slight crouch, weight on balls of feet
Lateral start — facing sideways, turn and sprint on signal
Back-pedal start — jog backward 2m then accelerate forward
Fall start — lean forward until falling, then sprint

test.name: Sprint consistency test
test.scoring: Best = peak speed · Average = consistency · S4/S5 significantly slower = insufficient recovery

blocks.label: Block 1 — Footwork & Beat
blocks.description: Synchronise foot contacts with a musical beat. Land on ball of foot.

exercises.name: Two-foot ankle bounce
cues: Bounce lightly in place, both feet together, one contact per beat
Ball of foot contact only
Arms loose, shoulders relaxed

exercises.name: Alternating foot tap
cues: Left tap beat 1 · right tap beat 2 · repeat
Lifted foot already loading for next tap
Stay on balls of both feet throughout

exercises.beat_pattern: in-L · in-R · out-L · out-R (4 beats = 1 rung)
cues: Both feet step inside (in-L, in-R) then outside (out-L, out-R)
4 contacts = 1 cycle · move up one rung per cycle
Quick and light

exercises.name: Lateral shuffle step
exercises.beat_pattern: step · step · ACCENT · step · step · ACCENT
cues: 3 cones in a line 2m apart
Shuffle A→B (3 steps) · accent on arrival
Accent = sharp weight shift, not a jump

diagram.label: Lateral shuffle step — accent on arrival at cone
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down view · 3 cones in a line</text><line x1='10' y1='96' x2='210' y2='96' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><polygon points='50,78 44,88 56,88' fill='#F2A623'/><text x='50' y='74' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>A</text><polygon points='110,78 104,88 116,88' fill='#F2A623'/><text x='110' y='74' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>B</text><polygon points='170,78 164,88 176,88' fill='#F2A623'/><text x='170' y='74' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>C</text><line x1='56' y1='55' x2='104' y2='55' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='80' y='50' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>3 shuffle steps</text><line x1='116' y1='62' x2='164' y2='62' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='140' y='57' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>3 shuffle steps</text><path d='M170,68 Q110,78 50,68' fill='none' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='4 2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='110' y='84' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#888'>return</text><text x='30' y='108' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>step · step · ACCENT</text><text x='132' y='108' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>step · step · ACCENT</text></svg>

exercises.name: Skip pattern
cues: HOP (accent, high knee) + step (quiet) alternating
Opposite arm drives up with each high knee
Feel the BIG-small · BIG-small pattern

diagram.label: Skip pattern — BIG hop + small step, 2-beat cycle
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Side view · skip forward</text><line x1='10' y1='96' x2='210' y2='96' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><path d='M20,92 Q32,60 44,92' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M44,92 Q52,80 60,92' fill='none' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1.2'/><path d='M60,92 Q72,60 84,92' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M84,92 Q92,80 100,92' fill='none' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1.2'/><path d='M100,92 Q112,60 124,92' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='62' cy='54' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='62' y1='61' x2='62' y2='78' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='62' y1='78' x2='72' y2='65' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='62' y1='65' x2='50' y2='55' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='62' y1='78' x2='55' y2='92' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><text x='32' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8' fill='#D85A30'>HOP</text><text x='52' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8' fill='#888'>step</text><text x='72' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8' fill='#D85A30'>HOP</text><text x='92' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8' fill='#888'>step</text><text x='112' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='8' fill='#D85A30'>HOP</text><text x='155' y='103' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>arm drives opp. knee</text></svg>

blocks.label: Block 2 — Body Rhythm
blocks.description: Add upper body, hip and whole-body coordination. Fluid connected movement.

exercises.name: Hip sway bounce
exercises.beat_pattern: L · ctr · R · ctr (hips on beats 1 and 3)
cues: Bounce lightly · shift hips left on beat 1, right on beat 3
Let hip shift happen naturally — follow the beat
Shoulders stay relaxed and level

diagram.label: Hip sway bounce — hips L/R on beats 1 and 3
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Front view · hips shift L-R on every beat</text><line x1='10' y1='100' x2='210' y2='100' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><circle cx='110' cy='35' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='110' y1='43' x2='110' y2='65' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='65' x2='100' y2='100' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='65' x2='120' y2='100' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><ellipse cx='110' cy='67' rx='14' ry='7' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.5'/><path d='M96,65 Q80,60 65,66' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><path d='M124,65 Q140,60 155,66' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='58' y='58' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>← sway</text><text x='140' y='58' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>sway →</text><text x='55' y='112' font-size='8' fill='#D85A30'>L</text><text x='80' y='112' font-size='8' fill='#888'>ctr</text><text x='105' y='112' font-size='8' fill='#3B8BD4'>R</text><text x='130' y='112' font-size='8' fill='#888'>ctr</text><text x='155' y='112' font-size='8' fill='#D85A30'>L</text></svg>

exercises.name: Arm-leg cross bounce
cues: Drive left knee up + right arm up simultaneously
Switch: right knee up + left arm up
This is the natural running pattern — feel the cross-body connection

diagram.label: Arm-leg cross bounce — contralateral L-knee/R-arm
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Front view · L knee + R arm · then R knee + L arm</text><line x1='10' y1='106' x2='210' y2='106' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><circle cx='110' cy='36' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='110' y1='44' x2='110' y2='68' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='68' x2='96' y2='56' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='96' y1='56' x2='92' y2='66' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='52' x2='128' y2='38' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='68' x2='124' y2='106' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='110' y1='52' x2='92' y2='62' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><text x='85' y='50' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>L knee ↑</text><text x='130' y='36' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>R arm ↑</text><text x='110' y='118' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>R knee + L arm next · alternate on every beat</text></svg>

exercises.name: 360° bounce turn
exercises.beat_pattern: Turn 90° per beat. 4 beats = 360°. Switch direction every 4 beats.
cues: Bounce and rotate 90° per beat
4 beats clockwise · 4 beats anticlockwise
Eyes track forward — feel the rotation

diagram.label: 360° bounce turn — 90° per beat, 4 beats = full circle
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down view · 4 beats = 360°</text><circle cx='110' cy='62' r='38' fill='none' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.8' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><circle cx='110' cy='24' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2'/><circle cx='148' cy='62' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='2 1'/><circle cx='110' cy='100' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='2 1'/><circle cx='72' cy='62' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2' stroke-dasharray='2 1'/><text x='110' y='18' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>start (beat 1)</text><text x='160' y='65' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>beat 2</text><text x='100' y='112' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>beat 3</text><text x='44' y='65' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>beat 4</text><path d='M118,24 Q148,35 148,62' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.4' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='148' y='42' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>rotate R</text><text x='110' y='68' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>one quarter-turn per beat</text><text x='110' y='80' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>switch direction every 4 beats</text></svg>

exercises.name: Wave arms walk
cues: Walk forward at beat pace
Every 2 steps, one arm makes a full wave (forward→up→behind→down)
Alternate arms · loose fluid movement

diagram.label: Wave arms walk — full arm wave every 2 steps
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Side view · walk with full arm wave every 2 steps</text><line x1='10' y1='100' x2='210' y2='100' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><circle cx='55' cy='38' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='55' y1='45' x2='55' y2='68' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M55,52 Q72,38 78,50 Q84,62 72,68' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.8' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='55' y1='52' x2='40' y2='62' stroke='#333' stroke-width='1.8' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='55' y1='68' x2='48' y2='100' stroke='#333' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='55' y1='68' x2='68' y2='88' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='140' cy='38' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2' opacity='.5'/><path d='M140,52 Q123,38 117,50 Q111,62 123,68' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='3 2' opacity='.6'/><text x='140' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4' opacity='.7'>L wave</text><text x='78' y='32' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>R wave</text><text x='110' y='112' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>walk-walk-WAVE · walk-walk-WAVE · fluid not mechanical</text></svg>

blocks.label: Block 3 — Tempo Change
blocks.description: Switch between slow, medium and fast movement patterns on demand. Most game-relevant block.

exercises.name: Slow-fast bounce
exercises.beat_pattern: SLOW × 8 → FAST × 8 → SLOW × 8. Instant gear change.
cues: 8 beats slow → 8 beats fast → repeat
Switch must be instant — not gradual
Same ankle stiffness at both speeds

diagram.label: Slow-fast bounce — instant gear change every 8 beats
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 105' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Side view · half-time then double-time</text><line x1='10' y1='90' x2='210' y2='90' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><path d='M15,86 Q27,50 39,86' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='2.2'/><path d='M39,86 Q51,50 63,86' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='2.2'/><text x='39' y='38' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>SLOW</text><text x='39' y='48' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>80 BPM</text><line x1='80' y1='30' x2='80' y2='90' stroke='#F2A623' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='80' y='25' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>switch!</text><path d='M80,86 Q86,65 92,86' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M92,86 Q98,65 104,86' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M104,86 Q110,65 116,86' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.8'/><path d='M116,86 Q122,65 128,86' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.8'/><text x='110' y='50' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>FAST</text><text x='110' y='60' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>160 BPM</text><line x1='145' y1='30' x2='145' y2='90' stroke='#F2A623' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='145' y='25' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>switch!</text><path d='M145,86 Q157,50 169,86' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='2.2'/><path d='M169,86 Q181,50 193,86' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='2.2'/><text x='175' y='48' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>SLOW</text><text x='110' y='100' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>8 beats slow → 8 beats fast → repeat · instant switch</text></svg>

exercises.name: Freeze and burst
cues: Move freely · accent = freeze
Hold freeze 2–3 beats · then burst in any direction
The pause IS the rhythm — silence is a beat too

diagram.label: Freeze and burst — musical accent = freeze then burst
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down view · move · freeze · burst on signal</text><line x1='10' y1='96' x2='210' y2='96' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><circle cx='30' cy='60' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1.2'/><line x1='37' y1='60' x2='75' y2='60' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='4 2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='55' y='55' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>dribble</text><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#F2A623' stroke-width='1.8'/><text x='90' y='64' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#F2A623'>✕</text><text x='90' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>FREEZE</text><rect x='74' y='44' width='32' height='14' rx='3' fill='none' stroke='#F2A623' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='90' y='54' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>pause</text><line x1='97' y1='60' x2='168' y2='60' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2.4' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='132' y='55' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>BURST on signal!</text><circle cx='175' cy='60' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='90' y='90' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11' fill='#F2A623'>♪</text><text x='110' y='90' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>= burst signal</text></svg>

exercises.name: Call and respond
cues: Player dribbles to background music
Coach calls a word · player responds immediately
Reaction must be on the next beat — not several beats later

diagram.label: Call and respond
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 165' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='11' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down view · free dribble area</text><rect x='20' y='20' width='180' height='120' rx='4' fill='none' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.8' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><path d='M80,110 Q95,75 110,90 Q125,105 140,70 Q155,50 130,50' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><circle cx='110' cy='85' r='9' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.2'/><text x='110' y='89' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#3B8BD4'>P</text><rect x='148' y='22' width='48' height='52' rx='4' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1'/><text x='172' y='34' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#D85A30'>CALLS</text><text x='172' y='44' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>slow · fast</text><text x='172' y='52' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>freeze · burst</text><text x='172' y='60' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>left · right</text><text x='172' y='68' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>turn</text><line x1='148' y1='48' x2='122' y2='80' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='110' y='150' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#aaa'>dribble to music · respond on the next beat</text><text x='110' y='160' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#aaa'>40 s on · 30 s rest · 2 sets</text></svg>

exercises.name: 3-tempo cone run
cues: 3 zones with colour-coded cones
Dribble through — change foot speed as you enter each zone
Transition must be instant — 2 steps in, already at new tempo

diagram.label: 3-tempo cone run — slow/medium/fast zones
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 115' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down view · 3 zones 3 tempos</text><line x1='10' y1='96' x2='210' y2='96' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.6'/><polygon points='25,72 20,82 30,82' fill='#1D9E75'/><polygon points='25,52 20,62 30,62' fill='#1D9E75'/><rect x='18' y='50' width='18' height='34' rx='2' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='27' y='44' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#1D9E75'>slow</text><polygon points='90,72 85,82 95,82' fill='#F2A623'/><polygon points='90,52 85,62 95,62' fill='#F2A623'/><rect x='83' y='50' width='18' height='34' rx='2' fill='none' stroke='#F2A623' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='92' y='44' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#F2A623'>medium</text><polygon points='175,72 170,82 180,82' fill='#D85A30'/><polygon points='175,52 170,62 180,62' fill='#D85A30'/><rect x='168' y='50' width='18' height='34' rx='2' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='177' y='44' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#D85A30'>fast</text><line x1='36' y1='62' x2='83' y2='62' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1.4' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><line x1='101' y1='62' x2='168' y2='62' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1.4' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><circle cx='10' cy='62' r='6' fill='none' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='1'/><text x='10' y='90' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>P</text><text x='110' y='108' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Instant tempo shift at each zone boundary</text></svg>

blocks.label: Block 4 — Ball & Rhythm
blocks.description: Ball as extension of rhythmic body movement. Eyes up, feel the ball.

exercises.name: Ball tap bounce
exercises.beat_pattern: bounce · bounce · TAP · bounce · bounce · TAP
cues: Ball on floor · bounce in place to the beat
Tap ball with inside of foot on every 3rd beat
Alternate feet · don't break bounce rhythm

diagram.label: Ball tap bounce — tap on every 3rd beat
diagram.svg:

exercises.name: Dribble to freeze
exercises.beat_pattern: dribble · dribble · FREEZE (2 beats) · dribble · FREEZE
cues: Dribble freely inside 2m square
Musical accent = freeze with ball under control
Head up — feel the music, don't watch the ball

exercises.name: Tempo dribble
cues: Slow dribble 8 beats → skill move → burst away
Slow phase = lure the defender in
This is Messi's pattern: slow · slow · BOOM

diagram.label: Tempo dribble — slow × 8 → skill → burst (Messi pattern)

exercises.name: Partner mirror dribble
roles: Follower (P2) mirrors every movement without ball
cues: Swap roles every 10 seconds — call the swap on a beat
P1 challenge: use rhythm changes to lose P2

sessions.title: Football Intelligence — Match Watching Session

focal_points.label: Movement off the ball
questions: Where does the player move when her teammate has the ball?
Does she create space for others or run into space for herself?
How many steps does it take her to get into position?
focal_points.insight: The best players spend 85% of the game without the ball. Watch what they do in that 85%.

diagram.label: Focal point A — movement off the ball
diagram.svg:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 95' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down · watch players WITHOUT the ball</text><rect x='15' y='18' width='190' height='70' rx='3' fill='none' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><circle cx='75' cy='52' r='7' fill='#F2A623'/><circle cx='75' cy='52' r='3' fill='#888'/><circle cx='130' cy='38' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M130,38 Q150,28 165,36' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='3 2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='155' y='26' font-size='6' fill='#3B8BD4'>run into space</text><circle cx='115' cy='72' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M115,72 Q135,78 150,68' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1.4' stroke-dasharray='3 2' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><text x='150' y='80' font-size='6' fill='#1D9E75'>check out</text><circle cx='38' cy='30' r='7' fill='none' stroke='#888' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='38' cy='30' r='3' fill='#888'/><text x='38' y='44' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#888'>watch here</text></svg>

focal_points.label: First touch & body position
questions: Is the player's body open or closed before she receives?
Does her first touch take her forward, sideways or backward?
How quickly can she play after her first touch?
focal_points.insight: Count how many times per half a player's first touch goes forward vs backward. Elite players: mostly forward.

diagram.label: Focal point B — first touch and body position

focal_points.label: Pressing & defensive shape
questions: When does the team start pressing — immediately or on a signal?
How compact is the defensive shape between players?
Do they force the opponent inside or outside?
focal_points.insight: Defending is a team shape, not individual battles. Watch the spaces between players, not just the ball.

diagram.label: Focal point C — pressing and defensive shape
diagram.svg:

<svg width='100%' viewBox='0 0 220 95' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><defs><marker id='arr' viewBox='0 0 10 10' refX='8' refY='5' markerWidth='5' markerHeight='5' orient='auto-start-reverse'><path d='M2 1L8 5L2 9' fill='none' stroke='context-stroke' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='12' text-anchor='middle' font-size='7' fill='#aaa'>Top-down · watch the spaces between defenders</text><rect x='15' y='18' width='190' height='70' rx='3' fill='none' stroke='#aaa' stroke-width='0.8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><circle cx='162' cy='52' r='6' fill='#D85A30'/><circle cx='162' cy='52' r='2' fill='#888'/><circle cx='138' cy='48' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='143' y1='48' x2='155' y2='51' stroke='#3B8BD4' stroke-width='1.4' marker-end='url(#arr)'/><ellipse cx='148' cy='60' rx='12' ry='6' fill='#3B8BD4' opacity='.1'/><text x='148' y='63' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#3B8BD4'>cover shadow</text><circle cx='115' cy='55' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='92' cy='44' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='88' cy='68' r='5' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='80' y='38' width='62' height='34' rx='3' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='0.8' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='111' y='82' text-anchor='middle' font-size='6' fill='#1D9E75'>compact defensive block</text></svg>

focal_points.label: Transitions
questions: When the team loses the ball, how fast do they react as a unit?
When they win the ball, do they play fast or slow?
Who reacts first in transition — and who reacts last?
focal_points.insight: The 3 seconds after possession changes are the most dangerous moments. The best teams react as a unit, instantly.

diagram.label: Focal point D — transitions (3 seconds after possession change)

during_tasks.name: The tally task
during_tasks.description: Count one specific action every time it happens in both halves

during_tasks.name: Shadow one player
cues: Watch ONLY chosen player for 5 full minutes
Count how many times she moves when ball is elsewhere
What does she do between touches?

during_tasks.name: Pause and rewind
cues: Pause → look at all 22 players' positions → unpause

during_tasks.name: Find the space
cues: Look away from ball — where is empty space?
Is anyone running into that space?
How did a player create space?

half_time.name: Half time retrospective
half_time.description: What happened during the first half time
questions: What formation is each team playing? Has it changed?
Which team dominates possession — is that helping them?
Where are chances coming from — left, right, centre, set pieces?
Which player has been most influential — why?
One thing to practise from what you saw in the first half
One mistake you saw that you also make — what caused it?
If you were coach, what one thing would you change?

after_stages.label: What did you see?
questions: Describe the match in 3 sentences — without mentioning the score
What tactical pattern did the winning team use most effectively?
Which player stood out — describe one specific action
How many times did your tally-task action happen each half?

after_stages.label: What did you learn?
questions: What did the shadowed player do that surprised you?
One thing you saw that you currently cannot do — want to learn
Did the match go as you predicted at half time?
One thing the losing team could have done differently

after_stages.label: What will you do?
questions: One specific thing to practise in next training session
Which training session would develop the quality you most admired today?
Which player do you want to watch again — what will you focus on?

recommended_matches: Barcelona Femení vs any top WSL club (movement, pressing, rhythm)
Chelsea Women vs Arsenal Women (pressing and defensive shape)
Any Champions League women's quarter-final or semi-final
Any Lamine Yamal match (dribbling rhythm and tempo change)
